description: A web-based SaaS invoicing platform tailored for freelancers to create, send, and track invoices and payments. It reduces administrative overhead by automating reminders, tracking payment status, and generating basic financial reports — replacing manual spreadsheet workflows for independent contractors.
features: Invoice creation with line items, taxes, and discounts
Client management (contact details, billing address, payment history)
PDF generation and email delivery of invoices
Payment status tracking (draft, sent, viewed, paid, overdue)
Automated payment reminder emails on configurable schedules
Multi-currency support with exchange rate display
Stripe/PayPal integration for online payment collection
Dashboard with revenue summary, outstanding balances, and aging report
Time tracking with conversion to billable line items
Tax report export (annual summary by client)
Multi-user team accounts (e.g., freelancer + accountant access)
Custom branding on invoices (logo, colors, payment terms)
Client self-service portal to view and pay invoices
mvpFeatures: Invoice creation with line items, tax rate, and discount fields
PDF generation and email delivery of invoices
Payment status tracking (draft, sent, paid, overdue) with manual mark-as-paid
Basic dashboard showing total outstanding, total paid (current month)
entities: Plan (subscription tier enum: free, pro, business)
userFlows: User signs up → chooses plan → verifies email → completes profile (business name, default currency, logo) → lands on empty dashboard with 'Create First Invoice' prompt
User creates invoice → selects existing client or creates new inline → adds line items with qty/rate → applies tax rate → previews PDF → sends via email → invoice status moves to 'sent'

Client receives email → clicks 'View Invoice' link (no login required) → views branded PDF in portal → clicks 'Pay Now' → completes Stripe checkout → invoice marked 'paid' → freelancer receives email notification

Invoice passes due date without payment → system moves status to 'overdue' → triggers reminder schedule → sends reminder emails at D+1, D+7, D+14 intervals → freelancer can disable reminders per invoice
User attempts to delete a client with existing invoices → warning prompt listing affected invoices → forced to either reassign invoices to another client or archive client instead of hard delete
Recurring template triggers on schedule → new invoice generated in 'draft' state → freelancer reviews and approves → invoice sent (or auto-sent if configured)
constraints: PDF generation must complete in under 3 seconds for invoices with up to 50 line items
API responses under 300ms p95 for dashboard and invoice list endpoints
Email delivery via transactional provider (SendGrid/Postmark) with delivery confirmation logging
Invoice data must be immutable once marked 'paid' — edits require a credit note workflow
Free plan capped at 5 active clients and 10 invoices/month enforced server-side
Invoice public view link must be accessible without authentication but scoped by signed URL token
Core invoice creation must be completable in under 5 user interactions
risks: PDF generation at scale: server-side rendering (Puppeteer/WeasyPrint) is CPU-heavy — needs async job queue (e.g., BullMQ + Redis) to avoid blocking API, adds infrastructure complexity from day one

Payment webhook reliability: Stripe webhook delivery is not guaranteed exactly-once — payment status updates require idempotency keys and deduplication logic to prevent double-marking invoices as paid

Multi-currency complexity: storing amounts in original currency vs. converted base currency creates reporting ambiguity — architectural decision needed upfront on whether to store both or normalize at write time

Email deliverability for invoice sends: freelancer's clients may have aggressive spam filters — SPF/DKIM must be configured correctly and sending domain reputation managed, or invoice delivery silently fails
openQuestions: Does the freelancer send invoices from their own email domain (requires custom SMTP or domain verification) or from a platform domain (e.g., invoices@freelanceinvoice.app)? This changes the email infrastructure architecture significantly.

Should payments collected via Stripe flow through a platform Stripe Connect account (enabling platform revenue share/fees) or directly into the freelancer's own Stripe account? Connect adds compliance complexity but enables monetization.

Is multi-language/locale support required for invoice templates (e.g., freelancers billing international clients who expect invoices in their language)? This affects the invoice rendering model and adds an i18n data layer.
